title: 3D-4 administrative droid
content:
{{Top|ga|leg}} {{DroidSeries |image= |name=3D-4 administrative droid |creator= |manufacturer=[[Genetech Corporation]]''[[Galaxy of Intrigue]]'' |designer= |homeworld= |line= |model= |class=[[Class three droid/Legends|Class three droid]] |degree= |cost=7,320 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] |length= |width= |height= |mass= |gender= |sensor= |plating= |armament=Concealed [[Hold-out blaster/Legends|hold-out blaster]] |equipment=*[[Recorder|Audio recorder]] *[[Holocamera/Legends|Video recorder]] |affiliation= |firstmade=Prior to [[26 ABY/Legends|26 ABY]]''[[The New Jedi Order: Edge of Victory I: Conquest]]'', which features a 3D-4 administrative droid, is set in [[26 ABY/Legends|26 ABY]] according to ''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' |retired= }} {{Quote|They've gone mad. They're destroying droids all over the city!''"
"''They aren't mad. They're just frightened. The Yuuzhan Vong are on Ando, and may well conquer the planet.''"
"''But why destroy droids, Master Dorsk?''"
"''Because the Yuuzhan Vong hate machines.|A 3D-4 droid and Jedi Knight Dorsk 82 on Ando|''[[The New Jedi Order: Edge of Victory I: Conquest]]''}} The '''3D-4 administrative droid''', also known as the '''3D-4 protocol droid''', was a [[Class three droid/Legends|class three droid]] produced by [[Genetech Corporation]] sometime before the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]]. Designed to compete in the [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]] market dominated by [[Cybot Galactica/Legends|Cybot Galactica's]] [[3PO-series protocol droid/Legends|3PO-series]], the 3D-4 sacrificed [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomatic]] protocols to focus solely on [[Merchant/Legends|business]] [[Administrator/Legends|administration]]. The [[Droid/Legends|droid]] was used mainly by businessbeings and [[Ambassador/Legends|ambassadors]], and served as a basic negotiator and [[Translator/Legends|translator]]. Although stock models were unarmed, the 3D-4 was capable of being refitted with a concealed [[Hold-out blaster/Legends|hold-out blaster]] in case of trouble. ==Description== [[Genetech Corporation]]'s 3D-4 [[Administrative droid/Legends|administrative droid]] was a [[Class three droid/Legends|third-degree]] bipedal unit, and also possessed two [[Limb/Legends|hands]]. It was equipped with a [[basic processor]], a [[Vocabulator/Legends|vocabulator]], a [[translator unit]] which was capable of understanding [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|basic]] and [[Binary/Legends|binary]] among other languages, and an encrypted internal [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]]. The 3D-4 was furnished with a large database containing information on [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Merchant/Legends|trade]], business and [[Lawyer/Legends|law]]. The [[Droid/Legends|droid]] possessed an internal storage capacity allowing it to store up to five [[Kilogram/Legends|kilograms]] of material, and was equipped with [[Recorder|audio]] and [[Holocamera/Legends|video recorders]]. Stock models were unarmed, but could be retrofitted to include a concealed [[Hold-out blaster/Legends|hold-out blaster]] at the owner's discretion. A license was required to own a 3D-4 [[Administrator/Legends|administrative]] droid, and they cost 7,320 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] to purchase. ==Role== The 3D-4 series was one of Genetech Corporation's answers to [[Cybot Galactica/Legends|Cybot Galactica's]] domination of the [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]] market by producing a droid more suited to administrative functions rather than [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomacy]]. Designed to assist organic beings with system management and administration duties, the 3D-4 was commonly employed by businessbeings and [[Ambassador/Legends|ambassadors]], along with those individuals who felt they needed a droid capable of operating in a variety of roles. The 3D-4's extensive knowledge of [[Bureaucrat/Legends|bureaucratic]] procedure, coupled with its translator and information database, allowed it to serve as a basic negotiator and [[Translator/Legends|translator]] during business dealings. Despite its administrative role, it was also referred to as the "3D-4 protocol droid." ==History== {{Quote|Go, please. Help the others board. My pilot is already at the controls with the flight instructions, so even if something happens to me, you'll be okay.''"
"''Why are you helping us, sir?''"
"''Because I am a Jedi and I can. You don't deserve destruction.|Jedi Knight Dorsk 82 and a 3D-4 on Ando|}} In [[26 ABY/Legends|26 ABY]], a [[Unidentified 3D-4 administrative droid|3D-4 droid]] was present on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Ando/Legends|Ando]] during the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]]. [[Extragalactic/Legends|Extra-galactic]] invaders, the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] despised machines as abominations and sought to destroy them all. As the Yuuzhan Vong arrived on Ando, droids were destroyed by the native [[Aqualish/Legends|Aqualish]] in an effort to appease the marauders. [[Dorsk 82]], a [[Khommite/Legends|Khommite]] [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] and [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]], sought to rescue as many droids as he could from what he saw as senseless destruction, and the 3D-4 was part of a group of thirty-eight droids that Dorsk 82 gathered together. As he guided the droids to his [[Dorsk 82's starship|ship]], the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] turned back to delay a group of Aqualish following them. Dorsk 82 attempted to placate the mob, but a [[Unidentified Aqualish security officer|security officer]] gave orders for the [[Starship/Legends|ship]] to be fired upon while it was still on the ground. The ship and its occupants—the 3D-4, thirty-seven other droids and Dorsk 82's [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]], [[Hhen]]—were [[Death/Legends|killed]] instantly. Dorsk 82 suffered the same fate shortly after as the Aqualish attempted to capture him to turn over to the Yuuzhan Vong. ==Behind the scenes== A 3D-4 droid first appeared in the prologue in [[Greg Keyes]]' ''[[The New Jedi Order: Edge of Victory I: Conquest]]'', which was part of the [[Star Wars: The New Jedi Order|''New Jedi Order'' series]] and published in [[2001]], in which it was referred to as a "3D-4 protocol droid." In [[2010]], [[Wizards of the Coast]]'s ''[[Galaxy of Intrigue]]'' profiled the 3D-4 administrative droid. This article assumes that the latter source's naming is the most [[canon]]. Despite similarities to the [[3D-4X administrative droid]]—including name, basic function and manufacturer—several differences are present between the models, chief among them the ability to refit the droid with a concealed weapon. The cost of the units are different, with 3D-4 droids costing 7,520 credits while 3D-4X droids retail for 5,500 credits.''[[Arms & Equipment Guide]]'' 3D-4 models are equipped with a basic processor, which means that the unit is not suited to independent problem-solving or creative thinking to carry out its tasks, and follows its instructions literally.''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game Saga Edition Core Rulebook]]'' In contrast, 3D-4Xs are built with a [[Heuristic processor/Legends|heuristic processor]] which allows the droid to learn by doing and formulate solutions to the problems it faces through logic and reasoning. The amount of internal storage capacity between the models also differs, with the 3D-4 capable of storing five kilograms within its internal compartments, while the 3D-4X is only capable of carrying two kilograms. For the purpose of this article, it is assumed that the 3D-4 and the 3D-4X are separate models due to these differences. ==Appearances== *''[[Maul: Lockdown]]'' {{Ab|Maul: Lockdown (audiobook)}} *{{FFG|story=Debts to Pay|book=Star Wars: Edge of the Empire Game Master's Kit|text=''Star Wars'': Edge of the Empire Game Master's Kit}} *''[[The New Jedi Order: Edge of Victory I: Conquest]]'' {{1st}} {{C|As 3D-4 protocol droid}} ==Sources== *''[[Galaxy of Intrigue]]'' {{1stID|3D-4 administrative droid}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Administrative droid models]] [[Category:Genetech Corporation products]] [[Category:Protocol droid models]]
